topic: Rust code analysis pipeline phases B-D: call graph, diagnostics, workspace topology, test output, unsafe detection, claim anchoring

sources.title: External Tools -- The Cargo Book
keyInsights: Canonical JSON schema for --message-format=json output
compiler-message, compiler-artifact, build-script-executed, build-finished reason types
package_id and target fields on every message

sources.title: JSON Output -- The rustc Book
keyInsights: Full diagnostic object schema with spans, children, rendered
All level values: error, warning, note, help, failure-note, ICE
suggestion_applicability: MachineApplicable, MaybeIncorrect, HasPlaceholders, Unspecified
Span fields: file_name, byte_start, byte_end, line_start, line_end, column_start, column_end, is_primary

sources.title: cargo metadata -- The Cargo Book
keyInsights: packages[], workspace_members[], workspace_default_members[], resolve, workspace_root, target_directory
source field discriminates path vs registry vs git deps
dependency kind: null=normal, dev, build
resolve.nodes[].deps[].dep_kinds for resolved dependency classification

sources.title: cargo_metadata crate -- docs.rs
keyInsights: Typed Rust parser for cargo metadata JSON output
MetadataCommand::new().run() returns Metadata struct
workspace_packages() iterator over workspace-member packages

sources.title: About output formats -- cargo-nextest
keyInsights: JUnit XML is the stable output format for test runs
libtest-json is experimental, schema documented as TODO
cargo nextest list --message-format json-pretty for test enumeration

sources.title: JUnit support -- cargo-nextest
keyInsights: testsuite name = test binary, testcase name = test function
time attribute is decimal seconds duration
failure, flakyFailure, rerunFailure child elements
Written to target/nextest/ci/junit.xml with --profile ci

sources.title: nextest-rs/quick-junit
keyInsights: Official JUnit library used by nextest itself
Always in sync with nextest JUnit schema
Report > TestSuite > TestCase hierarchy

sources.title: Libtest JSON output -- cargo-nextest
keyInsights: Experimental; requires NEXTEST_EXPERIMENTAL_LIBTEST_JSON=1
libtest-json and libtest-json-plus variants
Format spec marked as TODO in docs as of 2026-04
Version 0.1 as of 2023-12

sources.title: Finish the libtest json output experiment -- Rust Project Goals
keyInsights: libtest JSON is NOT yet stable as of 2026-04
Accepted 2025H1 project goal but stabilization work still in progress
Plan is to move reporting to Cargo level

sources.title: RFC 3558 libtest-json
keyInsights: The formal RFC for libtest JSON stabilization
Required reading to understand what schema is coming

sources.title: External Tools -- Criterion.rs Documentation
keyInsights: cargo-criterion --message-format=json emits benchmark-complete JSON per line
Fields: id, iteration_count, measured_values, unit, typical, mean, median, change
change.change: NoChange, Improved, Regressed
slope may be null; not all benchmarks support it
target/criterion JSON files are undocumented internals

sources.title: BurntSushi/critcmp
keyInsights: Compares criterion baselines; reads undocumented internal JSON
Useful for CI regression detection

sources.title: ra_ap_ide -- docs.rs
keyInsights: v0.0.329 as of 2026-04; published weekly; no semver guarantees
Exposes CallHierarchyConfig, CallItem, Analysis::call_hierarchy()
FindAllRefsConfig, ReferenceSearchResult for reference queries
Usable as library but 0.0.x means weekly breakage risk

sources.title: rust_analyzer::cli::scip
keyInsights: CLI: rust-analyzer scip <path> --output <path> --exclude-vendored-libraries
Outputs SCIP protobuf index
Batch analysis; no LSP handshake needed

sources.title: SCIP and LSIF Indexing -- rust-lang/rust-analyzer DeepWiki
keyInsights: StaticIndex::compute() analyzes whole project; produces TokenStaticData with references field
Moniker system: hierarchical identifiers for cross-repo navigation
LSIF: rust-analyzer lsif <path> to stdout as line-delimited JSON
SCIP: single index.scip protobuf file

sources.title: sourcegraph/scip-rust
keyInsights: Thin wrapper around rust-analyzer to generate SCIP data
Production pattern: shell to rust-analyzer scip binary
Used by Sourcegraph for production Rust code intelligence

sources.title: Beneficial-AI-Foundation/scip-callgraph
keyInsights: v5.0.0 with CI; builds call graph from SCIP JSON
Pipeline: rust-analyzer scip -> scip print --json -> graph extraction
Outputs DOT/SVG/D3/CSV formats
Rust + TypeScript codebase

sources.title: scip -- crates.io
keyInsights: v0.3.x; Rust SCIP protobuf parser
Use Index::parse_from_bytes() to deserialize index.scip

sources.title: rust-lang/project-stable-mir
keyInsights: Renamed to Rustc Librarification Project; crates named rustc_public
Nightly-only; no stability guarantees as of 2026-04
Goal: semver-stable API over MIR for external tools
Not viable for production today

sources.title: endorlabs/MIRAI
keyInsights: Abstract interpreter over Rust MIR
Supports --call_graph_config for call graph generation
Linter mode for finding unintentional panics
Taint analysis and constant-time analysis modes

sources.title: geiger-rs/cargo-geiger
keyInsights: cargo geiger --output-format=Json for machine-readable unsafe report
Tracks: unsafe functions, expressions, item_impls, item_traits, methods per crate
Issue #71 (undercounting) fixed in 0.12+

sources.title: cargo-geiger-serde -- docs.rs
keyInsights: SafetyReport, QuickSafetyReport, ReportEntry, UnsafeInfo, CounterBlock structs
Count struct tracks safe vs unsafe_ counts
Deserialize geiger JSON output with serde

sources.title: clippy undocumented_unsafe_blocks source
keyInsights: Detects BlockCheckMode::UnsafeBlock(UnsafeSource::UserProvided) on HIR Block nodes
check_block(), check_item(), check_stmt() entry points
Distinguishes user-written vs compiler-generated unsafe blocks
Adaptable pattern for tree-sitter or syn detection

sources.title: clippy multiple_unsafe_ops_per_block source
keyInsights: Counts distinct unsafe operations within a single unsafe block
Reference for understanding what constitutes an unsafe operation

sources.title: tree-sitter/tree-sitter-rust
keyInsights: v0.23.x (2025); handles Rust 2024 edition including unsafe extern blocks
unsafe_block, function_item with unsafe, impl_item with unsafe, extern_block
call_expression with scoped_identifier for concurrency primitive detection

sources.title: fix: Fix callHierarchy LSP violation -- rust-analyzer PR #17554
keyInsights: Fixes panic in callHierarchy/outgoingCalls
Pin rust-analyzer to post-2024-10 release to avoid this bug

sources.title: rust-analyzer panic when call callHierarchy/outgoingCalls -- Issue #17762
keyInsights: Documents the outgoing calls panic bug
Fixed in PR #17554

sources.title: LSP outside the editor -- Tom Selfin
keyInsights: Full Rust code example for subprocess LSP client
Command::new + stdin Stdio::piped + stdout Stdio::piped pattern
Content-Length header framing for JSON-RPC messages
Initialize -> initialized notification -> requests sequence

sources.title: Architecture -- rust-analyzer
keyInsights: stdout is used for LSP communication exclusively; println breaks the server
rust-analyzer is the only crate that knows about LSP and JSON serialization
handlers/request.rs implements all LSP requests

sources.title: Implementing a call graph generator as a cargo extension -- Rust Forum
keyInsights: April 2025: no mature production-ready call graph tool exists
Rupta, Crabviz, cargo-call-stack all have memory/compatibility issues
project-stable-mir recommended as best approach for new tools
Acceptable tradeoff: sacrifice call graph coverage for performance

sources.title: robinmoussu/cargo-callgraph
keyInsights: Abandoned; locked to a 2021 nightly rustc version
Do not use for new projects

sources.title: ra_ap_hir -- docs.rs
keyInsights: HIR provides high-level object-oriented access to Rust code
Bound to a particular crate instance with cfg flags and features applied
Alternative to MIR for source-level analysis

sources.title: rustc_public -- nightly rustc docs
keyInsights: rustc_public is the new name for stable-MIR API crate
Nightly-only; not on crates.io yet
Contains type definitions for external tools interacting with compiler

sources.title: A Context-Sensitive Pointer Analysis Framework for Rust -- ACM CC 2024
keyInsights: Rupta: context-sensitive pointer analysis on Rust MIR
On-the-fly call graph construction for Rust
Academic reference; not a production tool

sources.title: cargo message_format tests -- rust-lang/cargo
keyInsights: Canonical test cases showing expected JSON output shapes
Good reference for edge cases in message parsing

sources.title: fix: correct error count for cargo check --message-format json -- PR #14598
keyInsights: Recent fix to error counting in JSON mode
Confirms build-finished.success is reliable signal

sources.title: cargo-geiger -- crates.io
keyInsights: v0.13.0 published August 2025; v0.12.0 April 2025
Confirms active maintenance

sources.title: A benchmark for Rust call-graph generators -- Rust Internals
keyInsights: Historical evaluation of call graph tools; most are now outdated
Confirms no dominant production tool has emerged

sources.title: Call Graph of Monomorphized MIR Functions? -- Rust Forum
keyInsights: collect_crate_mono_items for monomorphized call graph entry points
TerminatorKind::Call is the MIR node to walk for call edges
